item.title: Building a Raspberry Pi 3B+ full keyboard handheld. Part 1
item.description: Not really surprisingly, it is quite hard to get a mobile Linux handheld device in 2019. You either have to build your own or try to get your hands on the (discontinued) obscure ones. Would computer manufacturers sell you a device that lasts you 10 years? It is not really profitable from the seller’s viewpoint. Owning a Linux handheld computer can you detox from harmful Social Media experiences; using FOSS also means getting rid of bloatware or walled gardens like Android, iOS, MacOS or Windows.

item.title: darkhttpd : you'll love static
item.description: darkhttpd - wiki is my favorite web server. It does one thing and it does it good: serve static files correctly. The overall best feature I’ve found in darkhttpd is that it is configured via command line arguments, so you can wrap the configuration of each one of your web servers into it’s runit/OpenRC service! Running a static webserver will help you reduce overhead and lets you reduce the resource usage of your services.

item.title: void-rice
item.description:
Hello world. I’ve decided to upload my suckless tools to github, so here’s void-rice. It can be barely called a desktop environment, but it features most things you need after a fresh install of Void Linux.
I’ve been using this setup for the last months and I’m really pleased by the native multihead (monitor) support with xrandr. void-rice features a custom patched version of the dwm window manager with system tray support and sane keybindings, dmenu, slstatus for the statusbar info and a patched st terminal emulator.

item.title: About
item.description: 0x0f0f0f I’m Alessandro, Computer Science student and programmer. I’ve worked to some full stack projects using different stacks, but I mostly use Node.JS for REST APIs. I’ve made some arcade games and I tinker a lot with anything UNIX. Right now I’m learning Haskell and I’m making a small LISP interpreter. Find my code at github.com/0x0f0f0f You can send me an email any time at sudo-woodo3 AT protonmail DOT com
